metadata.landingTitle: Visual AI Workflow Builder for Creators and Teams
metadata.landingDescription: Build AI workflows for prompts, image generation, video analysis, and reusable production pipelines in one visual canvas.
metadata.exploreDescription: Discover public AI workflows for image generation, video pipelines, prompt chains, and creator automation.
metadata.workflowsDescription: Browse reusable AI workflow templates for prompt engineering, image generation, video production, and multimodal automation.
metadata.videoAnalysisDescription: Upload videos and use AI to extract storyboards, scripts, shot notes, and production insights.

landingSeo.title: One visual AI workflow builder for prompt systems, image pipelines, and video analysis
landingSeo.intro: Nano Banana Canvas helps creators and teams design repeatable AI workflows in one visual canvas. Map prompts, connect models, share reusable templates, and keep production logic readable from idea to delivery.
landingSeo.coverageBody: The current product is built for distributed creator teams that need one shared workflow surface across time zones, languages, and production contexts.
landingSeo.coverageRegionAmericas: North America and remote US creator teams
landingSeo.coverageRegionEurope: European studios, agencies, and independent creators
landingSeo.coverageRegionApac: Asia-Pacific production teams and bilingual operators
landingSeo.capabilityBody: Use one canvas to connect prompt planning, image generation, video analysis, and reusable workflow templates without hiding logic inside fragmented tools.
landingSeo.geoBody: We describe real service scope, supported team setups, and working regions directly on the page instead of mass-producing thin location pages that only swap place names.
landingSeo.geoNote: That keeps the site aligned with Google guidance while still helping search engines and answer engines understand where the product is useful.
landingSeo.faqQuestion1: What is Nano Banana Canvas used for?
landingSeo.faqAnswer1: Nano Banana Canvas is a visual AI workflow builder for creators and teams who need reusable prompt chains, image generation steps, video analysis, and publishable workflow templates in one workspace.
landingSeo.faqQuestion2: Who is this workflow builder for?
landingSeo.faqAnswer2: It is designed for filmmakers, creative studios, agencies, and independent operators who need to coordinate AI-assisted production across different tools and time zones.
landingSeo.faqQuestion3: Does the platform support global teams?
landingSeo.faqAnswer3: Yes. The current product positioning explicitly supports distributed teams across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific, with English and Chinese product messaging available today.
landingSeo.structuredDescription: Visual AI workflow builder for creators and teams. Build prompt systems, image pipelines, video analysis flows, and reusable templates in one canvas.
landingSeo.featureWorkflow: Visual workflow builder for prompt systems and creator operations
landingSeo.featureImageVideo: Image generation, video analysis, and multimodal production pipelines
landingSeo.featureTemplates: Reusable workflow templates for teams and community sharing

hero.heading: Nano Banana Canvas
hero.tagline: Show the image logic before the image is even rendered.
hero.description: A face detail, a portrait base, a fused identity, a landscape, a placed scene, then a moving result. The canvas should make multimodal generation readable at a glance.
hero.models: Current runtime support is separated from ecosystem planning so the landing page stays honest.
hero.nodeFaceNote: Capture the inheritable facial structure first so the downstream portrait has a stable source of identity.
hero.nodePortraitNote: A second portrait provides the body, stance, and primary subject we want to keep.
hero.nodeFusionNote: The result reads as the second girl carrying the facial traits from the first source.
hero.nodeLandscapeNote: A real environment enters the graph as image context, not as another text label.
hero.nodeSceneNote: Now the fused character exists inside the selected environment with one coherent visual story.
hero.nodeMotionNote: The final state shifts from still composition into a moving cinematic result.

models.title: One canvas in the middle, multiple capability clusters around it.
models.description: The point is not to stack logos. The point is to show how text, image, video, and audio routing gather around a single production surface.
models.currentBody: What you see here separates current runtime support from broader ecosystem direction so the landing page proves capability without overstating integration reality.
models.clusterBody1: Prompt drafting, system structure, and planning logic stay readable before they ever enter the expensive generation path.
models.clusterBody2: Image workflows focus on fusion, scene continuity, and style transfer instead of one-off pretty outputs.
models.clusterBody3: Video routes are framed around multi-step production, where still frames become motion rather than isolated clips.
models.clusterBody4: Audio stays connected to the same graph, so voice or soundtrack output can live beside image and video logic.

features.title: The page should explain how the product works, not just declare that it exists.
features.description: Each feature panel carries a visual hint so the public page feels like a guided workflow system rather than a pile of dark cards and slogans.
features.cardBody1: Organize prompts, model steps, and production branches in one visible canvas so the graph stays comprehensible under pressure.
features.cardBody2: Combine identity, style, and scene inputs as explicit nodes, making the merge logic visible instead of hidden in one giant prompt.
features.cardBody3: Move from still composition to motion output through a staged workflow that feels like creative direction rather than a black box.
features.cardBody4: Switch between providers and capability clusters without turning the public surface into a fake all-in-one claim.

pricing.title: The homepage should help people decide, while the pricing page handles the transaction detail.
pricing.description: This section stays summary-level on purpose: enough clarity to compare paths, not enough duplication to fight the full `/pricing` page.

planBody.free: Open the canvas, validate the workflow shape, and only move into paid plans when credits or storage become real constraints.
planBody.standard: For solo builders who need a stable monthly production rhythm and enough room to turn experiments into repeatable workflows.
planBody.pro: For active creators and smaller teams that need stronger throughput without losing visibility into the graph itself.
planBody.ultimate: For heavier production teams that need a wider credit runway and room for larger asset libraries.

proof.title: If there is no verified customer quote yet, the page should prove usage patterns instead of inventing people.
proof.description: These cards are scenario proof and operating proof. They show where the product is useful, what it is built for, and how the public semantics line up with real platform intent.

faq.description: Questions should open on demand, with enough whitespace that the last section feels confident rather than cramped.

cta.title: The last screen should do one thing well: move people into creation.
cta.description: No extra noise here. The product has already explained itself by now. The final screen only needs to convert.
cta.body: Start from the landing page, enter the workflow space, and keep the graph understandable from first prompt to final output.

footer.tagline: A visual AI workflow surface for image, video, and multimodal production logic.
footer.copyright: © 2026 Nano Banana Canvas. All rights reserved.
footer.builtFor: Built for creators who want their workflow logic to stay visible.

features.metaDescription: See how Nano Banana Canvas structures visual workflow design, image fusion, video generation, and provider routing in one public feature overview.
features.title: A public surface for the workflow logic behind the product.
features.description: The point of this page is clarity. Each feature exists to make production structure readable, reusable, and easier to communicate across a team.
features.highlight1: Visual workflow is not decoration. It is the mechanism that keeps production logic inspectable across prompts, providers, and outputs.
features.highlight2: Image fusion turns identity, style, and scene into explicit building blocks so a result can be reasoned about instead of guessed at.
features.highlight3: Video generation needs visible continuity from still frames into motion, so creative direction does not vanish between iterations.
features.highlight4: Model routing keeps the product honest about what is wired now and what belongs to the larger ecosystem plan.

visualWorkflow.metaDescription: Understand how Nano Banana Canvas turns prompts, branches, models, and outputs into an inspectable visual workflow.
visualWorkflow.title: A workflow should be something a team can inspect, not a hidden chain of guesses.
visualWorkflow.description: Visual Workflow is the surface that keeps production logic readable. Prompts, branches, providers, and outputs stay visible as connected decisions instead of disappearing into a single fragile instruction.
visualWorkflow.highlight1: Nodes make each production step explicit, so a team can see what happened before a model call spends credits or changes the result.
visualWorkflow.highlight2: Branching turns alternatives into structure instead of chat history. The canvas keeps options comparable and reusable.
visualWorkflow.highlight3: Outputs remain attached to their upstream context, which makes iteration easier to explain, audit, and repeat.

imageGeneration.metaDescription: See how Nano Banana Canvas frames image generation as visible identity, style, and scene composition rather than isolated prompt guessing.
imageGeneration.title: Image generation works better when identity, style, and scene are separate enough to reason about.
imageGeneration.description: The image surface is designed around visible composition. A creator can keep character traits, references, and environments separate before merging them into a result that still has a readable lineage.
imageGeneration.highlight1: Face and identity references can become explicit inputs instead of being buried inside one overloaded prompt.
imageGeneration.highlight2: Style and scene references stay visible as separate production material, which makes fusion easier to revise.
imageGeneration.highlight3: Generated results remain connected to their source decisions, so a team can refine the chain instead of restarting from scratch.

videoGeneration.metaDescription: Explore how Nano Banana Canvas explains still-frame to motion workflows for AI-assisted video production.
videoGeneration.title: Video generation needs continuity, not just another beautiful still frame.
videoGeneration.description: The video workflow page explains how still images, motion intent, and model output can be organized as a production chain. The goal is to make motion decisions visible before they become expensive iterations.
videoGeneration.highlight1: Keyframes give motion a visible starting point, so the team can discuss what should remain stable across the sequence.
videoGeneration.highlight2: Motion prompts and provider choices stay attached to the visual source instead of drifting into separate notes.
videoGeneration.highlight3: Results can be compared as stages in a chain, which keeps iteration grounded in the same creative direction.

modelRouting.metaDescription: Learn how Nano Banana Canvas presents multi-provider routing while keeping runtime support and ecosystem planning honest.
modelRouting.title: Model routing should make provider choice explicit, not hide it behind vague automation.
modelRouting.description: Model Routing explains how the canvas can separate workflow intent from provider execution. It keeps current runtime support visible while leaving room for a broader ecosystem without pretending everything is already wired.
modelRouting.highlight1: Provider choices become part of the workflow graph, so a result can be traced back to the model path that produced it.
modelRouting.highlight2: Text, image, video, storage, and future model routes can be described as capability lanes instead of a disconnected logo wall.
modelRouting.highlight3: The public page keeps the boundary between real runtime support and ecosystem direction explicit, which protects trust.

models.metaDescription: Explore the current runtime support and broader model ecosystem around Nano Banana Canvas across text, image, video, and audio.
models.title: Current support where it is real. Ecosystem breadth where it is directional.
models.description: This page exists so people can inspect the model story without forcing the homepage to carry every provider detail at once.
models.highlight1: Text and reasoning providers shape planning, prompt structure, and graph control before generation cost is spent.
models.highlight2: Image and video providers are framed as production clusters, not as disconnected logo walls.
models.highlight3: The distinction between runtime support and planned ecosystem scope stays explicit so the public narrative remains trustworthy.

docs.metaDescription: Open the public documentation entrance for Nano Banana Canvas to understand the product structure, workflow surface, and public resource paths.
docs.title: The documentation surface should help people orient themselves before they enter the product.
docs.description: This page is a public doorway for structure, not a dead-end placeholder. It points people toward workflow concepts, models, and the community surface.
docs.highlight1: A clear docs entrance reduces the pressure on the homepage by giving deeper product context its own public location.
docs.highlight2: Public docs help navigation, search clarity, and team onboarding at the same time.
docs.highlight3: As the landing tree grows, docs becomes the stable resource layer instead of another hidden dropdown item.

about.metaDescription: Learn what Nano Banana Canvas is trying to build for creators and teams working across prompts, image pipelines, and video workflows.
about.title: The product is trying to make creative workflow logic visible, not mystical.
about.description: Nano Banana Canvas is shaped around one simple belief: if the production graph is readable, a team can think more clearly, share more honestly, and iterate with less waste.
about.highlight1: The product story is grounded in visible workflow structure rather than hidden prompt magic or vague automation claims.
about.highlight2: The design direction favors cinematic restraint, readable motion, and public pages that explain how the tool thinks.
about.highlight3: As the system grows, the public surface should still feel coherent enough that a new visitor can understand the core idea in a few screens.

pricing.metaDescription: View Nano Banana Canvas subscription pricing with live Stripe-backed prices and region-aware currencies.
pricing.title: Choose the plan that matches your production volume
pricing.description: Plan prices are loaded from Stripe at request time so the UI stays aligned with the real billing configuration instead of drifting behind hard-coded numbers.
pricing.livePriceNote: Displayed currency is resolved automatically from your region and Stripe price configuration.
pricing.freeEyebrow: Free is the default entry state
pricing.freeTitle: Start on Free first. Upgrade only when your production volume actually grows.
pricing.freeDescription: Free is not a checkout product. It is the account state you land in by default, so you can enter the canvas, validate the workflow, and only move into paid plans when monthly credits or storage become the real bottleneck.
pricing.freeFeatureEntryBody: Every new account starts here by default.
pricing.freeFeatureCreditsBody: Upgrade only when credits or storage become the constraint.
pricing.freeFeatureUpgradeBody: There is no “buy Free” button because Free is not a Stripe SKU.
pricing.billingNoticeBody: Auto monthly plans renew until you cancel. One-time plans and credit packs are charged once and do not create a recurring subscription.
pricing.refundNoticeBody: Unused one-time plans or credit packs are not promised to be refundable by default. Any exception is handled case by case under our Terms and Stripe payment records.
pricing.currencyNoticeBody: Displayed currency is resolved from your region and Stripe configuration, but the final charge currency and amount shown in Stripe Checkout at payment time control the order.
pricing.standardDescription: For solo creators who need a stable monthly credit pool and room for production assets.
pricing.proDescription: For active operators and small teams shipping more image, video, and workflow output each month.
pricing.ultimateDescription: For heavier production teams that need a much larger credit budget and longer storage runway.

auth.loginSubtitle: Return to the canvas and continue the same creative line you already started.
auth.registerSubtitle: Open an account layer that feels native to the same canvas instead of a second disconnected tool.
auth.visualHeadline: Balloons rise into a grainy night while the product waits in silence.
auth.visualDescription: The left side should feel like a premium poster, not a requirements dump. Let the scene hold the atmosphere while the right side handles identity.
auth.visualPointOne: Poetic brand language belongs here because this panel is about mood, not interface instruction.
auth.visualPointTwo: The visual should suggest lift-off, reflection, and distance, as if the account surface is opening into a wider creative night.
auth.visualPointThree: The product stays restrained: black, silver, grain, glow, and one large handwritten brand mark on the form side.
auth.termsPrefix: By creating an account you agree to our

workspace.deleteWarning: This will permanently delete "{name}" and all its data. This action cannot be undone.

profile.accountPageDesc: Manage profile, works, notifications, and API connection cards for each capability.
profile.accountLoginRequiredTitle: Sign in to save account-level API configs
profile.accountLoginRequiredBody: These configs are now bound to your real account for cross-device access and per-user secret isolation. Guest sessions no longer write this kind of account asset.
profile.name: Name
profile.guestStatus: You are still in guest mode
profile.guestStatusBody: After signing in, your works, tasks, and account-level configs will be bound to your real account so visitor data does not bleed across users.
profile.signedInStatus: Your real account session is now active
profile.signedInStatusBody: From this point on, new account-level resources are bound to your real user record. Signing out returns you to a safe public page.
profile.manageBillingBody: If you already bought a plan, use this entry to open Stripe Customer Portal and manage your subscription, payment method, and billing details.
profile.apiConfigDesc: Maintain API connection configs for text, image, video, and audio. Nodes in API Key mode will read from these cards.
profile.apiConfigSecurityBody: These cards store API connection configs for each capability. Secrets are encrypted before they are written to the database and are read only within the current visitor context.
profile.noApiConfigs: No configs yet. Add one first.
profile.saveHint: After saving, matching nodes can use this config in API Key mode.
profile.capability_text_desc: Manage API providers and models used by text-generation nodes.
profile.capability_image_desc: Manage API providers and models used by image-generation nodes.
profile.capability_video_desc: Manage API providers and models used by video-generation nodes.
profile.capability_audio_desc: Manage API providers and models used by audio-generation nodes.

billing.title: Track credits, usage, and subscription state in one place
billing.description: This page reads from the local billing mirror so you can inspect balances, credit ledger history, and model usage before jumping into Stripe Customer Portal.
billing.balanceDescription: Your local billing mirror keeps monthly, permanent, and frozen credit pools separate so the account state stays auditable.
billing.historyDescription: Showing the latest {pageSize} entries out of {total} credit ledger events.
billing.usageDescription: A {days}-day summary built from AI execution logs and model pricing snapshots.

profileWorks.title: My Works
profileWorks.localDraftTitle: A local draft was found on this device
profileWorks.localDraftBodySignedIn: We found the local draft "{name}" with {count} nodes. You can import it into your real account workspace now.
profileWorks.localDraftBodyGuest: We found the local draft "{name}". Sign in to import it into your real account so it does not stay stranded on this device.
profileWorks.importDescription: Imported from the current device local draft
profileWorks.importSuccess: "{name}" has been imported into your account workspace
profileWorks.importFailed: Failed to import the local draft

legal.terms_acceptance_body: By accessing or using Nano Banana Canvas, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our services.
legal.terms_services_body: Nano Banana Canvas provides a visual AI workflow orchestration platform that allows users to build, share, and run AI-powered creative workflows. We reserve the right to modify or discontinue any part of the service at any time.
legal.terms_accounts_body: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials and for all activities under your account. You must provide accurate and complete information when creating an account and keep it up to date.
legal.terms_content_body: You retain ownership of content you create using our platform. By publishing content, you grant us a non-exclusive license to display and distribute it within the platform. You are solely responsible for ensuring your content does not violate any laws or third-party rights.
legal.terms_access_body: We make reasonable efforts to keep the service available, but we do not guarantee permanent availability of any feature, model, or third-party provider. We may change, suspend, or remove capabilities for security, compliance, cost, or product reasons.
legal.terms_billing_body: Nano Banana Canvas offers a default Free account state, auto-monthly subscriptions, one-time plans, and one-time credit packs. Free is not a Stripe checkout product. Paid products only affect the billing entitlements we describe in-product, including monthly credits, permanent credits, and storage allowances.
legal.terms_refunds_body: Unless required by applicable law, auto-monthly subscriptions remain active through the paid billing period after cancellation and are not retroactively refunded for the current cycle. One-time plans and credit packs are generally treated as consumed digital billing value once provisioned to your account. Refund requests may be reviewed case by case for duplicate charges, technical provisioning failures, or other exceptional billing errors.
legal.terms_currency_body: Prices may be displayed in different currencies based on your region and Stripe price configuration. Those display values are informational until checkout. The final payable currency, taxes, and amount are the values shown in Stripe Checkout or the Stripe-hosted billing surface at the time you confirm payment.
legal.terms_termination_body: We may suspend or terminate your account if you violate these terms. You may delete your account at any time. Upon termination, your data will be deleted in accordance with our Privacy Policy, while completed billing records may be retained for legal, accounting, fraud-prevention, or dispute-resolution purposes.
legal.terms_liability_body: Our platform is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the service. Our total liability shall not exceed the amount you paid in the preceding 12 months.
legal.privacy_collection_body: We collect information you provide or generate while using the service, including workflow content, usage data, and technical data such as browser type, IP address, and device identifiers. When you configure account-scoped API keys, we store the minimum encrypted configuration data required to make that setup available across devices.
legal.privacy_usage_body: We use your information to provide and improve our services, process transactions, send service-related communications, prevent fraud, and comply with legal obligations. We do not sell your personal data to third parties.
legal.privacy_billing_body: When you start a paid checkout, we share the minimum data needed to create or manage the order with Stripe, such as your account identifier, billing plan or package selection, Stripe customer reference, and payment outcome metadata. We do not store raw card numbers on our own servers. Billing records, invoice references, and transaction identifiers may be retained to support entitlements, refunds, accounting, fraud prevention, and dispute handling.
legal.privacy_sharing_body: We share your information only with service providers who help us operate (such as Cloudflare and Stripe), when required by law, or when you choose to publish content publicly. Published workflows display your username and avatar.
legal.privacy_security_body: We implement industry-standard security measures including encryption in transit (TLS) and at rest. API keys used for account-scoped execution are encrypted before they are stored on the server and are only read within the current signed-in account context. We regularly review our security practices, but no networked service can promise absolute security.
legal.privacy_cookies_body: We use essential cookies for authentication, session management, and checkout continuity. We use analytics to understand how our platform is used. You can control cookie preferences through your browser settings, although disabling certain cookies may affect sign-in or billing flows.
legal.privacy_rights_body: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. You can export your workflows at any time. To delete your account and most associated data, visit your account settings or contact our support team. Some billing and compliance records may be retained when required by law or for legitimate business obligations.
legal.privacy_contact_body: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at privacy@nanobanana.com. We will respond to your inquiry within 30 days.
